description: A medium-sized damselfly associated with the Nile River system. Males have blue and black coloration. The species is named after the Nile River, which flows through the core of its range.
behaviour: Found along the Nile River and its tributaries, perching on waterside vegetation and rocks.
distribution: *Pseudagrion niloticum* occurs along the Nile River system in Sudan, Ethiopia, and Uganda.
habitat: Flowing streams and rivers, particularly along the Nile drainage.
sources: Atlas of the European Dragonflies and Damselflies, Jean-Pierre Boudot(Editor), Vincent J Kalkman(Editor), Fons Peels(Illustrator)
Field guide to the dragonflies of Britain and Europe, Klaas-Douwe B Dijkstra.
